surface.name: Agora Video SDK Web Quickstart through a two-client basic video call
surface.selection_basis: Agora's current documentation presents Video Calling, Voice Calling, Interactive Live Streaming, Chat, Signaling, Whiteboard, and AI as distinct top-level products without establishing one global first surface. The Video SDK Quickstart is nevertheless a complete candidate: its metadata says it builds a basic Video Calling app, its structured tabs set Web as the canonical platform, and its test section supplies a two-client completion route.

alternatives_considered.name: Voice Calling
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer realtime-media product and the platform-level docs do not rank it below Video Calling.

alternatives_considered.name: Interactive Live Streaming
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer product for host and audience workflows rather than the selected all-participants Video Calling route.

alternatives_considered.name: Chat
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer messaging surface with its own SDK quickstart and account enablement path.

alternatives_considered.name: Android Video SDK
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer platform tab. The current structured Quickstart identifies Web as canonical and Web avoids a mobile IDE and physical-device build.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create an Agora account and secured project, then build and run the official Web Video SDK sample until two clients join the same channel and display realtime video.
assumed_prior_state: No Agora account, project, App ID, token, Vite project, SDK installation, source files, browser client, channel, or prior usage is assumed.

Node.js, npm, two camera-and-microphone clients or one client plus the official Web demo, a supported browser, and permission to grant media access are available; no signup, policy acceptance, verification, project creation, token generation, package installation, or call was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Build a basic Video Calling app with the Agora Video SDK.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Two Web Video SDK clients have joined the same Agora channel with the same App ID, channel name, and temporary token, published their local audio and video, and displayed local and remote video.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Each client displays its local video; after the peer publishes, the registered `user-published` handler subscribes and plays the remote track, while the first client's console logs `Publish success!` after its local publish completes.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The Quickstart test uses two clients in the same channel, and the complete sample logs `Publish success!` and renders local and remote video containers.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Quickstart defines a basic Video Calling app as initialization, channel join, and sending and receiving audio and video. The two-client test exercises all three before the guide moves to security and production next steps.

prerequisites.requirement: Create an Agora account with email, phone number, or a third-party account. The email route requires a verification code plus name, company name, phone number, and password.
Read and accept the Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, and Acceptable Use Policy during account signup.
Create an Agora project with a project name, use case, and Secured mode: APP ID + Token (Recommended).
Copy the project's App ID and generate a temporary RTC token for a chosen channel name.
Have Node.js and npm, and use a supported browser; Agora strongly recommends the latest stable Google Chrome.
Have a camera and microphone plus two devices, or use the documented official Web demo as the second client.
The current free package includes 10,000 participant minutes and suspends service at overuse, so payment is not a first-call prerequisite while free minutes remain.

primary_path.action: Open the Video SDK Quickstart with the Web platform selected and follow its Agora account management prerequisite.
primary_path.output: The Web prerequisites, project setup, implementation, complete sample, UI, and test sequence are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The page describes a basic Video Calling app and Web is the canonical structured platform.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A platform-level product choice remains unresolved because Agora exposes several peer products.

primary_path.action: Open the Agora signup page and choose the email signup route.
primary_path.output: The email registration form is available.
primary_path.success_signal: Required fields and the policy checkbox can be completed.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Phone-number and third-party signup are alternative routes.

primary_path.action: Fill the required email-signup fields, read the three linked policies, select the agreement checkbox, and click Continue.
primary_path.input: Required signup information and policy acceptance.
primary_path.output: Agora sends or requests an account verification code.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid or missing fields prevent continuation; detailed validation rules are left to the screen.

primary_path.action: Enter the verification code and click Confirm.
primary_path.input: The verification code received for the account.
primary_path.output: Agora requests the remaining account profile and password.
primary_path.success_signal: The name, company, phone, and password continuation is shown.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The account waits for a valid code.

primary_path.action: Follow the on-screen instructions to provide name, company name, phone number, and password, then click Continue.
primary_path.input: The requested profile and password values.
primary_path.output: The account is created and automatically logged in.
primary_path.success_signal: Agora Console opens and guides the user toward a first project.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: On-screen field rules must be satisfied.

primary_path.action: Open Projects in Agora Console and click Create New.
primary_path.success_signal: Project name, use case, and authentication mechanism can be entered.

primary_path.action: Enter a project name and use case, select Secured mode: APP ID + Token (Recommended), and click Submit.
primary_path.input: Project metadata and the recommended secured authentication mode.
primary_path.output: Agora creates the project and lists it on Projects.
primary_path.success_signal: The new project appears with an App ID.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The project requires the on-screen fields to be complete.

primary_path.action: Copy the App ID from the project's App ID column.
primary_path.output: The unique App ID is available for the sample.
primary_path.success_signal: The App ID has been copied.

primary_path.action: Edit the project, click Generate Temp Token in Security, enter a channel name, click Generate, and copy the RTC token.
primary_path.input: A chosen channel name for the first call.
primary_path.output: A temporary RTC token bound to the channel is available.
primary_path.success_signal: The token is generated and copied.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The same channel name must later be used by both clients.

primary_path.action: Create the documented vanilla JavaScript Vite project.
primary_path.output: The `agora_web_quickstart` folder contains a vanilla Vite project.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Package download or scaffold errors stop setup.

primary_path.action: Enter the new project, install its dependencies, and install the Agora Web SDK.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Package installation failures must be resolved before coding.

primary_path.action: Create or replace the JavaScript source with the complete sample code, including SDK import, client initialization, event listeners, join and leave handlers, local track creation and publishing, and local and remote playback containers.
details: Keep the sample's `user-published` subscription handler and `Publish success!` log.
primary_path.input: The complete JavaScript sample from the Quickstart.
primary_path.output: The application's JavaScript implements the basic call lifecycle.
primary_path.success_signal: The source contains `startBasicCall()` and references Join and Leave controls.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Omitted event listeners can cause published peer media to be missed.

primary_path.action: Replace the sample connection placeholders with the copied App ID, the selected channel name, and the generated temporary token; leave uid as 0 for a generated user ID.
primary_path.input: App ID, channel name, and temporary token.
primary_path.output: The source is configured for the secured test channel.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A mismatched channel name invalidates the temporary token for the test.

primary_path.action: Replace the root `index.html` with the documented Web SDK Video Quickstart UI containing Join and Leave buttons and the module script for `/src/main.js`.
primary_path.output: The page can load the call code and expose its controls.
primary_path.success_signal: Join and Leave elements have the IDs expected by the JavaScript.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Mismatched IDs prevent the sample handlers from attaching.

primary_path.action: Start the Vite development server with `npm run dev`.
primary_path.output: Vite prints a local URL such as `http://localhost:5173`.
primary_path.success_signal: The development server remains running and the URL is displayed.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A port conflict or build error prevents the URL from serving.

primary_path.action: Open the printed localhost URL, click Join, and grant camera and microphone access if the browser requests it.
primary_path.input: The first configured client and browser media permission.
primary_path.output: The client joins, creates local tracks, publishes them, displays local video, and logs `Publish success!`.
primary_path.success_signal: The local video container is visible and the console contains `Publish success!`.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Denied permission, invalid credentials, unsupported browser behavior, or non-localhost HTTP access can block media or connection.

primary_path.action: On a second device, repeat installation and launch, or open the official Web demo, and configure it with the same App ID, channel name, and temporary token.
primary_path.input: A second supported client with matching channel credentials.
primary_path.output: The peer is ready to join the same channel.
primary_path.success_signal: The second client shows its Join control with matching values.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Using different credentials or an expired token prevents the clients from meeting.

primary_path.action: Click Join on the second client and verify that each client receives and plays the other's published video and audio.
primary_path.input: Two joined clients in the same secured channel.
primary_path.output: Both clients participate in the basic realtime video call.
primary_path.success_signal: Local and remote video are displayed, and the remote track arrives through the `user-published` subscription handler.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the peer does not appear, verify matching App ID, channel, token, media permission, supported browser, and HTTPS or localhost requirements.

candidate_paths.name: Secured Web Video SDK Quickstart with two clients
candidate_paths.selection_basis: This candidate follows the Web tab's recommended secured project, complete JavaScript sample, Vite setup, and explicit second-client test without substituting an undocumented shortcut.
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: It is the fully instantiated Video Calling candidate recorded in the primary path; human judgment is still required because Agora does not designate Video Calling as the platform-wide default over its other products.

steps.action: Open the Web Video SDK Quickstart and its Agora account management prerequisite.
steps.success_signal: Web setup and testing sections are available.

steps.action: Choose email signup, fill the required fields, accept the three policies, and click Continue.
Enter the verification code, click Confirm, provide name, company name, phone number, and password, then click Continue.
steps.output: The account is created and logged in.
steps.failure_or_wait: A valid code and screen constraints are required.

steps.action: Open Projects, click Create New, enter a project name and use case, select Secured mode: APP ID + Token (Recommended), and click Submit.
steps.failure_or_wait: Required project values must be valid.

steps.action: Copy the App ID, edit the project, generate a temporary RTC token for a chosen channel name, and copy it.
steps.output: App ID and token are available.
steps.failure_or_wait: The channel name must be reused exactly.

steps.action: Run `npm create vite@latest agora_web_quickstart -- --template vanilla` and enter the created folder.
Copy the complete JavaScript sample into the source file, preserving initialization, event listeners, join and leave, track publication, and local and remote playback.
steps.success_signal: The source includes the Join handler and `Publish success!`.
steps.failure_or_wait: Missing event listeners can lose peer media events.

steps.action: Insert the App ID, channel name, and temporary token into the sample connection parameters.
steps.output: The first client targets the secured channel.

steps.action: Replace `index.html` with the documented Join and Leave UI and module-script reference.
steps.output: The controls match the JavaScript IDs.
steps.success_signal: The page can attach both handlers.

steps.action: Run `npm run dev` and keep the Vite server running.
steps.failure_or_wait: Build or port errors prevent serving.

steps.action: Open the URL, click Join, and grant camera and microphone permission.
steps.output: Local media is published and displayed.
steps.success_signal: Local video appears and `Publish success!` is logged.
steps.failure_or_wait: Permission or credential errors block the join.

steps.action: Prepare a second installed client or the official Web demo with the same App ID, channel, and token, then click Join.
steps.output: A peer publishes into the same channel.
steps.failure_or_wait: Different or expired credentials prevent connection.

steps.action: Verify that both clients display local and remote video and play subscribed audio.
steps.output: The basic Video Calling app is working.
steps.success_signal: Each side receives the other's media.
steps.failure_or_wait: Check credentials, permissions, browser support, and localhost or HTTPS requirements.

branches.path: Follow that product's own account enablement, credential, SDK, and first-success guide.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the meaning and terminal of the platform's first mile, which is why platform-level human judgment remains required.

branches.path: Select the provider on the Console login page, complete verification, click Create a new account, accept the policies, and click Continue.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces email verification and profile-password steps before converging on Console.

branches.condition: Add to an existing Web project
branches.path: Create a JavaScript source in `src`, add an HTML UI, and include it with a module script instead of scaffolding Vite.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Skips new-project initialization but preserves SDK, credentials, and call behavior.

branches.path: Use Agora's official Web demo or clone the official API Examples Web project as the peer.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Provides the documented second participant without duplicating the local scaffold.

friction_gates.description: Account creation requires accepting three named policies.
The email route requires a received verification code before profile completion.
The account guide recommends secured App ID plus Token mode, and production should use a token server rather than temporary tokens.
The first call requires an App ID, channel name, and a temporary token generated for that channel.
Camera and microphone permission is required to create and publish local media tracks.
The full receive-and-display test requires a second client, although the official Web demo can substitute for a second local install.
The free package suspends service after its 10,000 included participant minutes rather than charging overage.

sources.title: Agora account management | Agora Docs
sections_used: Sign up for an Agora account

sources.title: Real-Time Engagement & Conversational AI Platform | Agora
evidence_supported: Absence of one platform-wide first product

uncertainties.question: Which Agora product should define a platform-wide first mile: Video Calling, Voice Calling, Interactive Live Streaming, Chat, Signaling, Whiteboard, or an AI surface?
uncertainties.impact: Each product has different resources, credentials, implementation steps, and success terminals.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official platform and docs expose peer product families without naming one global default; this record supplies Video Calling as a complete candidate, not an assumed universal answer.

excluded_after_success: Leaving the channel is a cleanup operation after the two-client success.
Production token-server deployment, cloud proxy, encryption, optimization, recording, analytics, UI polish, deployment, and scaling occur after the basic Video Calling app works.
